_comment: EDITORIAL LAYER — Kimley-Horn Nuclear Task Force. This file is hand-maintained and is authoritative over the auto-scraped list. The weekly job pulls the *list* of restriction states from NCSL/DOE; everything below (mechanism wording, engagement notes, momentum flags, and any tier corrections) comes from here. To override a state, add or edit its entry. Keys: tier (restricted|partial|clear) forces the tier; partial=true designates a geographic/site-specific restriction; mech = short mechanism label; note = panel detail; recent=true flags a recently-opened market; effective = date string for recent changes.

CA.note: 50-yr moratorium; bars new fission plants until a federal high-level waste reprocessing/disposal solution is approved.

HI.note: State constitution requires a two-thirds vote of both legislative houses to authorize a new nuclear facility.

ME.note: Requires majority approval at statewide election plus PUC certification of an operational federal waste-disposal pathway.

MA.note: Requires statewide ballot approval plus legislative certification of a federally approved waste pathway and emergency plan.

MN.note: 1994 statutory ban on new reactor construction (Minn. Stat. 216B.243). Repeal bills (e.g. SF350) introduced 2025, in committee — watch for movement.

OR.note: Long-standing ban; requires an operational federal HLW repository and a statewide vote of approval.

RI.note: Siting/construction of new nuclear facilities requires General Assembly approval (reaffirmed May 2025).

VT.note: New nuclear construction requires legislative approval.

CT.note: Construction barred statewide EXCEPT the Millstone site (2022 exemption). 2025 Act 25-173 lets municipalities opt-in to advanced reactors by local vote.

NY.note: No statewide restriction. Moratorium applies only within the former LILCO service area — Nassau, Suffolk, and parts of Queens. Toggle County Detail to view.

NJ.note: De facto CAFRA waste-disposal moratorium repealed Apr 8 2026 (S3870/A4528, P.L.2026 c.9). State Nuclear Task Force established under EO 2.

IL.note: Moratorium rolled back 2023 (300 MW cap); size cap removed Jan 2026 — now open to all reactor classes.

KY.note: Moratorium repealed in 2017; Kentucky Nuclear Energy Development Authority established.

WI.note: First state to repeal its moratorium (2016).
